text: The surgeon performs an inward T-laparotomy, revealing a tumor located in the upper part of the liver. A polycystectomy is carried out, and the hepatic pedicle is detached from the hepatic parenchyma at the hyaluronan region.
The surgeon ligates and cuts the cystic structure, taps the right hepatic pedicle, and encircles and clamps the right anterior pedicle.

The right anterior segment of the liver shows a color change, and its borderline is marked. The infrahepatic part of the inferior vena cava is tapped and clamped. The Pringle maneuver is performed. Several hanging sutures are applied. The liver parenchyma is dissected between the left medial segment and the right anterior segment, with small veins to the middle hepatic vein being ligated and capped.

The surgeon identifies and clamps several glistening branches to Segment A, causing the segment to become cyanotic. The branches are ligated and cut, ensuring the tumor in the resecting part has an adequate cross margin. The liver parenchyma is dissected along marked demarcation lines, and small veins to the right hepatic vein are ligated and cut. Following the resection of Segment A, a co-shaped hole is visible in the upper part of the liver.
